behavior_summary: This script implements the object's 'U8P_BIGUGLY::look' (examine) handler. When the relevant actor inspects the object, the handler configures per-process display/state flags, invokes the item's bark routine with the literal string "tree ", and then suspends.
Before calling bark the code writes a per-object/info key and applies a 'process exclude' mark (inferred to alter how the object is reported or excluded from some processing lists).
The bark call returns a value which the routine captures into a local temporary, but that return value is not used for further logic in this snippet.
A short ephemeral string "tree " is allocated/passed to Item::bark and then freed by the handler.
notable_control_flow: Entry as a process-style handler (suspendable): perform two engine ops to set info and mark process-exclude, build literal string, push parameters, call Item::bark, free the string, capture return into a temp, then suspend/return.
No branching or loops are present in the observed snippet — single linear flow for the 'U8P_BIGUGLY::look' event.
Side-effect ordering: info/process-exclude writes happen before the Item::bark call (implying they influence how the bark/display is treated).
open_questions: What is the concrete meaning and numeric key of the 'info' opcode used (what display/metadata field is being set)? (inferred, not explicit)
Exactly what effect 'process exclude' has on engine processing and which subsystems consult it (rendering, AI, listing, save state)?

Who is the intended recipient of Item::bark here — the item process itself presenting text, or the inspecting player's process (PID) — the disassembly suggests the object calls its bark but the target semantics are inferred.
Are there any other side effects (events triggered elsewhere) when setting the info/process-exclude flags that are not visible in this snippet?

main_function.name: U8P_BIGUGLY::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the object's examine/U8P_BIGUGLY::look event by preparing object state and emitting a short descriptive message ('tree ').
entry_conditions: Event 0 / 'U8P_BIGUGLY::look' — triggered when the Avatar or another actor inspects or looks at the object (area-entry/interaction event).

function_map.name: U8P_BIGUGLY::look
function_map.role: Process-style event handler for the object's U8P_BIGUGLY::look/examine action; sets per-object info/process-exclude and calls Item::bark with the literal "tree ".

external_calls.purpose: Cause the item/process to emit or display a short one-line description; here invoked with the literal string "tree ".

writes.name: info (per-object/info key)
writes.likely_effect: Write a metadata/display/info field on the object to alter how it is reported or processed (exact key/value inferred).

writes.name: process exclude
writes.likely_effect: Mark the object excluded from some processing lists or iterations (prevents further default handling by subsystems).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the object's 'U8P_BIGUGLY::look' (examine) handler. When the relevant actor inspects the object, the handler configures per-process display/state flags, invokes the item's bark routine with the literal string "tree ", and then suspends.
